pr.title: Fix context generation and support Linux in `m2ctx`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Fixed several header/context-generation problems and added Linux support to tools/m2ctx by running the mwcc preprocessing command through wine on Linux. The PR mostly removed duplicate or misplaced declarations, moved toward canonical includes, fixed a global symbol type, made enum values explicit after removing shared AS_FTCOMMON entries from fighter action enums, and corrected a missing return type in mplib.h.
what_changed: Added platform detection to tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py and prepended `wine` to the mwcc preprocessing command when `platform.system() == 'Linux'`.
Removed a duplicate or incorrect `sprintf` declaration from include/MSL.h, leaving the typed declaration in include/stdio.h.
Removed the local `FILE` typedef from include/stdio.h.
Changed `OS_THREAD_QUEUE` in include/dolphin/os/OSThread.h from `OSThreadQueue` to `OSThreadQueue*` while preserving the non-M2CTX address annotation at 0x800000DC.
Removed many GX TEV-related extern declarations from src/dolphin/gx/GX_unknown_001.h, apparently because they belong in GXTev.h instead of the unknown GX header.
Changed multiple character-specific fighter action enums so their first character-specific action is explicitly assigned 341, removing the local `AS_FTCOMMON = 340` entry from those enums.
Converted src/melee/ft/chara/ftZelda/ftzelda.h from `#pragma once` to a conventional include guard `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTZELDA_FTZELDA_H`.
In src/melee/gm/gmmain_lib.h, added `#include <common_structs.h>` and removed a local `UnkFlagStruct` definition, implying reuse of a shared definition.
In src/melee/mp/mplib.h, added the missing `BOOL` return type to the `func_800509B8` declaration.
smart_moves: The Linux support is minimal and targeted: it only wraps the existing mwcc command with `wine` on Linux instead of changing the compiler flags or context-generation pipeline.

Removing duplicated standard-library declarations from MSL.h, stdio.h, and string.h reduces conflicts during preprocessing and makes generated context more dependent on the intended canonical headers.
Moving TEV declarations out of GX_unknown_001.h and including GXTev.h in GXInit.c improves header ownership: GX_unknown_001.h stays narrower while GXInit.c still sees the needed TEV declarations.
Assigning the first character-specific fighter action to 341 preserves numeric action IDs after removing `AS_FTCOMMON = 340` from individual character enums.
Replacing a local flag union definition in gmmain_lib.h with an include of common_structs.h avoids duplicate struct/union definitions in generated context.
decomp_lessons: For m2ctx-generated context, duplicate declarations across project headers can be as harmful as missing declarations; prefer one canonical declaration site and include it where needed.
When removing shared enum sentinel values such as `AS_FTCOMMON`, explicitly pin the next value if downstream code depends on exact numeric IDs.
Headers used by context generation should avoid compiler-specific conveniences when they can confuse tooling; conventional include guards are safer than relying on `#pragma once` in some contexts.

Global address annotations under `#ifndef M2CTX` can coexist with context generation, but the declared type still needs to match the actual symbol shape, as shown by changing `OS_THREAD_QUEUE` to a pointer.
If a header function prototype lacks a return type, context generation and C parsing can fail or infer the wrong type; fix prototypes even when the underlying function body is not part of the PR.
naming_conventions: Fighter action enum names use the `AS_<CHARACTER>_<ACTION>` pattern, e.g. `AS_FOX_SPECIALN_START`, `AS_MARIO_UNK1`, and `AS_NESS_ATTACKS4`.
The removed shared fighter action enum entry was named `AS_FTCOMMON`; this PR avoided redefining it inside character-specific enums.
Zelda's include guard was named from the path in uppercase: `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTZELDA_FTZELDA_H`.
Dolphin GX headers are split by subsystem names such as `GXAttr.h`, `GXGeometry.h`, `GXLight.h`, `GXTev.h`, `GXTransform.h`, and `GX_unknown_001.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic is documented in comments, but the header fixes are context-generation tactics: remove duplicate prototypes, include the correct subsystem header, and make enum values explicit to keep numeric constants stable.

The `OS_THREAD_QUEUE` declaration retains the fixed address annotation outside M2CTX while hiding it from M2CTX, a useful pattern for symbols that need real addresses in source but valid parseable declarations in generated context.
Linux support for context generation uses `wine mwcc ... -E` rather than requiring a native mwcc executable.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further refactor GX_unknown_001.h into named GX subsystem headers.
Find PRs that standardize fighter action enum numbering around AS_FTCOMMON and character-specific actions starting at 341.
Find PRs modifying tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py for cross-platform preprocessing or mwcc invocation.
Find PRs that centralize standard-library declarations in include/MSL.h, include/stdio.h, or include/string.h.
